title: Microstylis cochleariifolia
intro: Microstylis cochleariifolia, also known as the lip orchid, is a flowering plant species belonging to the orchid family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Microstylis cochleariifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly prepared as a powder or decoction for the treatment of various ailments, and is also used in the production of herbal teas, capsules, and ointments.
taxonomy: Microstylis cochleariifolia is a species of orchid, belonging to the family Orchidaceae. The genus Microstylis is part of the subfamily Orchidoideae. Microstylis cochleariifolia is native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. It is classified as a terrestrial orchid, typically found in moist, shaded areas. The classification of this orchid species is further detailed in the botanical nomenclature system.

The plant has been utilized in the treatment of various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.
